observation: Recursive chunking produces sentence-aligned chunks; fixed-size is faster and more deterministic. Precision delta: +0.000
Larger chunks carry more context per vector, improving faithfulness. Smaller chunks are more precise but may miss surrounding context. Precision delta: -0.053
Higher overlap reduces boundary effects where a sentence spans two chunks, at the cost of more redundant vectors and a larger index. Precision delta: +0.027
Increasing k provides more context to the LLM, improving recall but increasing prompt length and latency. Precision@k delta: -0.051
all-MiniLM is fine-tuned on diverse semantic similarity pairs; paraphrase-MiniLM targets paraphrase detection. Performance may differ on domain-specific corpora. Precision delta: -0.007
